rationale: In the literature review, the citation is not just part of a long list: the authors summarize a specific mechanism from Barasch and Berger (larger audiences reduce sharing of negative information due to self-presentation concerns). They use that mechanism to motivate why online review environments may show positive imbalance and to set up their own polarity self-selection account. The paper explicitly says it builds on this stream, even though it does not directly re-test the cited paper's audience-size manipulation.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Barasch and Berger (2014) as a conceptual foundation for the argument that broad audiences in online contexts can suppress negative sharing. It then builds its own framework on top of that idea to explain cross-platform variation in review polarity via polarity self-selection.
